commentary: Four activities against last week's twelve — okay, so we are absolutely not doing that again, are we. The swims dropped from 2000m sessions down to 1500m and the heart rate came in calmer, which honestly tracks when you're running on less volume and probably a body that needed the rest after that absolutely unhinged week where you doubled up nearly every single day. The chest-and-shoulders and back-and-legs sessions showing up on Thursday and Friday is the lift program holding its shape even when everything else went quiet, and that matters. One week you're logging a 13.3km Saturday run and a "recovery" run that was clocking 150 bpm — sweetheart, that is not a recovery run — and the next week your body basically went on a polite general strike, so let's treat this as the deload it was and come back next week like the very sexy, very capable man you are.
metrics_commentary: Okay, three kilometres in the pool with zero runs to show for it — you are a creature of the water this week and honestly the chlorine era is serving you. Your HRV jumped to 62 against a seven-day average of 51, which means your body is recovering well and quietly thriving, probably because swimming is doing exactly what it should. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.7 is genuinely impressive and worth feeling smug about, but the running shoes are gathering dust, babe, and at some point the legs need to remember they exist. Get them out before they forget how to be hot.
gym_commentary: Two out of four sessions this week, which is the kind of number that would be embarrassing if the bench press hadn't just hit a new PR of 35.5 kg estimated one-rep max, up from 34.8, because that is your chest getting stronger whether the week cooperated or not. Volume came in at 13,705 kg against last week's 20,148 kg, so yes, the gap is real and worth being honest about, but a gap week with a PR in it is not a lost week. The strength is still moving in the right direction — quad at 168 kg, hinge at 107.9 kg, that back pulling 84 kg on the lat pulldown — the foundation is absolutely there. Get back in for the other two sessions he doesn't know he owes himself yet.
